resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def lastSubstring(self, s: str) -> str:
        max_str = s[-1]
        for i in range(len(s) - 1, 0, -1):
            if s[i] > max_str[-1]:
                max_str = s[i:] + max_str[:i]
            elif s[i] == max_str[-1]:
                if s[i:i+2] > max_str[-2:]:
                    max_str = s[i:i+2] + max_str[:i]
        return max_str
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates over the string from right to left, maintaining the maximum substring seen so far. It compares each character with the last character of the current maximum substring, and updates the maximum substring if a larger one is found. This approach ensures that the maximum substring is always the last one in lexicographical order.
